verses.dev: तस्य तद्वचनं श्रुत्वा सीता रौद्रस्य रक्षसः।आर्ता दीनस्वरा दीनं प्रत्युवाच शनैर्वचः
verses.trans: On hearing the demon's haughty words, oppressed, pitiable Sita replied slowly in a sorrowful tone.

verses.dev: दुःखार्ता रुदती सीता वेपमाना तपस्विनी।चिन्तयन्ती वरारोहा पतिमेव पतिव्रतातृणमन्तरतः कृत्वा प्रत्युवाच शुचिस्मिता।
verses.trans: Sita, austere, beautiful and chaste afflicted by grief kept sobbing. Brooding over her husband and trembling in fear, she placed a blade of grass in between her and Ravana and replied with a gentle smile on her face.

verses.dev: निवर्तय मनो मत्तः स्वजने क्रियतां मनःन मां प्रार्थयितुं युक्तं सुसिद्धिमिव पापकृत्।
verses.trans: "Turn your mind away from me and fix it on your wives. You should not expect to seek me just as a sinner cannot hope to seek blessedness.

verses.dev: अकार्यं न मया कार्यमेकपत्न्या विगर्हितम्कुलं सम्प्राप्तया पुण्यं कुले महति जातया।
verses.trans: "I was born of high lineage and joined a meritorious family by virtue of my marriage. I am devoted only to my husband. I should not commit this contemptible, forbidden act."

words.m: a woman devoted to a single husband

verses.dev: एवमुक्त्वा तु वैदेही रावणं तं यशस्विनीराक्षसं पृष्ठतः कृत्वा भूयो वचनमब्रवीत्।
verses.trans: Glorious Vaidehi having thus spoken to the demon turned her back and continued:

verses.dev: नाहमौपयिकी भार्या परभार्या सती तवसाधु धर्ममवेक्षस्व साधु साधुव्रतं चर।
verses.trans: "I cannot be your lawful wife obtainable by your personal efforts. I am a chaste woman married to another man. (Being a king) examine the dharma honestly and adopt a pious way of life.

verses.dev: यथा तव तथान्येषां दारा रक्ष्या निशाचरआत्मानमुपमां कृत्वा स्वेषु दारेषु रम्यताम्।
verses.trans: "O nightranger Enjoy your wives. Take care of them. Taking your example, others' wives also deserve to be protected.

verses.dev: अतुष्टं स्वेषु दारेषु चपलं चलितेन्द्रियम्नयन्ति निकृतिप्रज्ञं परदाराः पराभवम्।
verses.trans: "The wives of others will insult an evilminded man who is not satisfied with his own wives, is fickleminded and has no control over his senses.

words.m: one with no control over the senses

verses.dev: इह सन्तो न वा सन्ति सतो वा नानुवर्तसेतथाहि विपरीता ते बुद्धिराचारवर्जिता।
verses.trans: "Are there no pious men around or are you not heeding their words? Your mind is perverted and inclined to commit a forbidden act.

words.m: that which is a forbidden act

verses.dev: वचो मिथ्याप्रणीतात्मा पथ्यमुक्तं विचक्षणैःराक्षसानामभावाय त्वं वा न प्रतिपद्यसे।
verses.trans: "You are indulging in unrighteous act and not listening to the advice of wise men who have a sense of discrimination. You are the cause of the destruction of demons.

verses.dev: अकृतात्मानमासाद्य राजानमनये रतम्समृद्धानि विनश्यन्ति राष्ट्राणि नगराणि च।
verses.trans: "Even prosperous countries and cities are destroyed on account of indiscreet rulers engaged in unfair means.

verses.dev: तथेयं त्वां समासाद्य लङ्का रत्नौघसङ्कुलाअपराधात्तवैकस्य नचिराद्विनशिष्यति।
verses.trans: "Having got you as its ruler, this Lanka filled with gems (riches) will perish without delay for the crime of one individual.

verses.dev: स्वकृतैर्हन्यमानस्य रावणादीर्घदर्शिनःअभिनन्दन्ति भूतानि विनाशे पापकर्मणः।
verses.trans: "O Ravana living beings will welcome and enjoy the destruction of a shortsighted sinner killed by his own vile act.

verses.dev: एवं त्वां पापकर्माणं वक्ष्यन्ति निकृता जनाःदिष्ट्यैतद्व्यसनं प्राप्तो रौद्र इत्येव हर्षिताः।
verses.trans: "Seeing you, getting ruined this way for your sin, those who were angry with you for their humiliation in your hands will feel glad and say 'Luckily this terrible fellow has met with his doom' .

verses.dev: शक्या लोभयितुं नाहमैश्वर्येण धनेन वाअनन्या राघवेणाहं भास्करेण प्रभा यथा।
verses.trans: "I cannot be lured by your power or wealth. I am inseparable from Raghava like light from the Sun.

verses.dev: उपधाय भुजं तस्य लोकनाथस्य सत्कृतम्कथं नामोपधास्यामि भुजमन्यस्य कस्य चित्।
verses.trans: "How can I pillow on somebody's arm after resting on the honourable arm of the great lord of the worlds ?

words.m: of the lord of the people

verses.dev: अहमौपयिकी भार्या तस्यैव वसुधापतेःव्रतस्नातस्य विप्रस्य विद्येव विदितात्मनः।
verses.trans: "I am alone the lawful wife of the lord of the earth like the knowledge of the Vedas which rightfully belongs to a wise brahmin who has realised the self after having had his ceremonial bath (as a token of celibacy) and having observed strict vows during the period of his study.

words.m: an ascetic who had ceremonial bath having observed vows
of the lord of the earth

verses.dev: साधु रावण रामेण मां समानय दुःखिताम्वने वाशितया सार्धं करेण्वेव गजाधिपम्।
verses.trans: "O Ravana it is proper for you to unite me in distress with Rama as one would unite a female elephant with the lordly elephant from which it is separated in the wilderness.

verses.dev: मित्रमौपयिकं कर्तुं रामः स्थानं परीप्सतावधं चानिच्छता घोरं त्वयासौ पुरुषर्षभः।
verses.trans: "I wish you to build friendship with Rama, the bull among men. Surely it is the right thing for you to do, if you are desirous of avoiding a dreadful death.

verses.dev: विदितः स हि धर्मज्ञः शरणागतवत्सलःतेन मैत्री भवतु ते यदि जीवितुमिच्छसि।
verses.trans: "He is famous as a knower of dharma and is compassionate to those who seek his protection. If you desire to preserve your life build friendship with him.

words.m: kind to those who seek his protection

verses.dev: प्रसादयस्व त्वं चैनं शरणागतवत्सलम्मां चास्मै नियतो भूत्वा निर्यातयितुमर्हसि।
verses.trans: "Propitiate him. He is compassionate to those who seek refuge. Exercise control over your senses for your sake and it is proper for you to return me to him.

words.m: one who is compassionate to those who seek refuge

verses.dev: एवं हि ते भवेत्स्वस्ति सम्प्रदाय रघूत्तमेअन्यथा त्वं हि कुर्वाणो वधं प्राप्स्यसि रावण।
verses.trans: "It is good for you to give me back to Rama, the foremost in the Raghu dynasty. Otherwise you will face death.

verses.dev: वर्जयेद्वज्रमुत्सृष्टं वर्जयेदन्तकश्चिरम्त्वद्विधं तु न सङ्कृद्धो लोकनाथः स राघवः।
verses.trans: "A thunderbolt may spare its target, Yama, lord of death may spare for a long time the life of a person doomed. But Rama, the lord of the world, will not spare any one when enraged.

verses.dev: रामस्य धनुषः शब्दं श्रोष्यसि त्वं महास्वनम्शतक्रतुविसृष्टस्य निर्घोषमशनेरिव।
verses.trans: "You will hear the terrible twang of Rama's bow resembling the rumble of the thunderbolt released by Indra who performed a hundred yajnas.

words.m: released by Indra who has performed a hundred yajnas

verses.dev: इह शीघ्रं सुपर्वाणो ज्वलितास्या इवोरगा: इषवो निपतिष्यन्ति रामलक्ष्मणलक्षणाः।
verses.trans: "Arrows marked with Rama and Lakshmana will soon rain on Lanka like the poisonous snakes with flaming fangs.

words.m: marked with the names of Rama and Lakshmana

verses.dev: रक्षांसि परिनिघ्नन्तः पुर्यामस्यां समन्ततःअसंपातं करिष्यन्ति पतन्तः कङ्कवाससः।
verses.trans: "The shafts bound with feathers of Kanka birds (released by Rama and Lakshmana) will be killing the demons all over this Lanka. They will not give a chance for any other weapon to fall.

words.m: the shafts with feathers of Kanka bird

verses.dev: राक्षसेन्द्रमहासर्पान् स रामगरुडो महान्उद्धरिष्यति वेगेन वैनतेय इवोरगान्।
verses.trans: "Just as Garuda swiftly extirpates the serpents, Rama will exterminate the demon leaders.

words.m: great serpents (in the form of demon chiefs)

verses.dev: अपनेष्यति मां भर्ता त्वत्तः शीघ्रमरिन्दमःअसुरेभ्यः श्रियं दीप्तां विष्णुस्त्रिभिरिव क्रमैः।
verses.trans: "My husband, the subduer of enemies, will soon take me away just as Lord Visnu in the form of Vamana took away the glowing prosperity from the demons with three strides.

verses.dev: जनस्थाने हतस्थाने निहते रक्षसां बलेअशक्तेन त्वया रक्षः कृतमेतदसाधु वै।
verses.trans: "When the army of demons as killed you had no capacity to protect and was destroyed at Janasthana, the place of death. You acted in this dishonest way.

verses.dev: आश्रमं तु तयोः शून्यं प्रविश्य नरसिंहयोःगोचरं गतयोर्भ्रात्रोरपनीता त्वयाधम।
verses.trans: "You are a mean creature. Finding me in the vacant hut, when the two brothers, lions among men, were away, you abducted me.

verses.dev: न हि गन्धमुपाघ्राय रामलक्ष्मणयोस्त्वयाशक्यं संदर्शने स्थातुं शुना शार्दूलयोरिव।
verses.trans: "Had you smelt the presence of Rama and Lakshmna, it would not have been possible for you to stand there, like a dog under the gaze of a pair of tigers.

verses.dev: तस्य ते विग्रहे ताभ्यां युगग्रहणमस्थिरम्वृत्रस्येवेन्द्रबाहुभ्यां बाहोरेकस्य विग्रहे।
verses.trans: "You will not be able to withstand them in a battle. You will meet the same fate as the onearmed Vritra in a battle with the twoarmed Indra.

verses.dev: क्षिप्रं तव स नाथो मे रामः सौमित्रिणा सहतोयमल्पमिवादित्यः प्राणानादास्यते शरैः।
verses.trans: "My lord Rama accompanied by Saumitri will draw out your life by his arrows swiftly as Aditya sucks up shallow water at once by his radiance.

verses.dev: गिरिं कुबेरस्य गतोऽथ वालयं सभां गतो वा वरुणस्य राज्ञः।असंशयं दाशरथेर्न मोक्ष्यसे महाद्रुमः कालहतोऽशनेरिव
verses.trans: "Even if you fly to Kubera's mountain (Kailasa) or the residence of king Varuna (ocean) you will not escape Rama's arrows like a mighty tree cannot evade lightning. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये सुन्दरकाण्डे एकविंशस्सर्गः। Thus ends the twentyfirst sarga of Sundarakanda of the holy Ramayana, the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.
